subagent_outcome.summary: The local supervisor invoked the auditor subagent and produced validator-clean artifacts, but final decisions still required supervisor repair to satisfy the deterministic gate mapping.

bridge_hardening_applied.reason: The bridge initially treated PowerShell Set-Content commands that wrote assigned runtime report files as extra terminal commands. The verifier now classifies assigned report writes and runtime Test-Path existence checks as benign while still flagging repeated materializer commands and writes outside allowed runtime report paths.

token_costs.not_usable_reason: The end checkpoint was taken after post-run bridge hardening, focused tests, deterministic validation, and reparse work. The span is useful as a broad upper-bound trace but not as clean Copilot-supervisor run economics.

interpretation: V6 is the best process evidence for split_02 so far: the local Copilot supervisor ran the one-shot materializer once, invoked the subagent, wrote both required reports, ran deterministic validators, avoided delete commands, and produced a bridge-accepted candidate after the bridge verifier was fixed to allow assigned report-file writes. The quality caveat remains: the subagent result still needed supervisor repair, so this is a supervisor-orchestration win rather than a clean subagent-audit win.
